name: mx-kv
description: Local key-value store for fast agent state (string, counter, list, history, and state types).

env_vars.MX_CURRENT_AGENT: Selects which agent's per-agent KV namespace is read or written.
env_vars.MX_HOME: Root of the mx data directory; the KV schema and data files live underneath it.

pathways.name: check-state
pathways.description: Discover which keys exist and read a current value.

args.name: key
args.description: Name of the key to read after listing the schema.

steps.note: List every defined key with its type.
Read the current value of a string/counter/state key.

pathways.name: track-history
pathways.description: Append an entry to a history/list key and read the most recent entries back.

args.name: key
args.description: Name of the history/list key to append to and read back.

args.name: value
args.description: Entry text to append to the key.

steps.note: Append a timestamped entry (use --create history to auto-create the key).
Read the last N entries to confirm the append.

pathways.name: adjust-counter
pathways.description: Read a counter, increment it, and read it back.

args.name: key
args.description: Name of the counter key to read and increment.

steps.note: Increment the counter (use --by N for a larger step).

errors.description: The requested key, entry index, or hash does not exist.
errors.resolution: Run 'mx kv keys' to list valid keys, or push an entry with --create to auto-create the key.

errors.description: The operation is not valid for the key's type (e.g. 'inc' on a string, 'pop' on a history).
errors.resolution: Check the key's type with 'mx kv keys' and use a read/write verb that matches it.

errors.description: The key exists in the schema but has no data yet.
errors.resolution: Set or push a value before reading.

keys.description: List all defined keys with their types and descriptions.
keys.agent_description: Enumerate every KV key with its type before reading or writing — the cheapest way to orient. Output is a plain text table; there is no JSON mode.

examples.description: List every key and its type.
examples.note: Run this first when you do not already know a key's type. There is no --json flag; the output is a text table of key and type.

get.description: Get the current value of a key, or specific entries by ID.
get.agent_description: Read a string/counter/state value, or specific list/history entries via --id.

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Entry ID (35), range (35-64), or comma-separated IDs (1,5,12). Formats cannot be combined.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display the linked memory entry (kn- reference).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

fields.name: value
fields.description: For scalar keys (string/counter/state) the value is returned directly under "value"; for list/history keys the result is instead an array of entry objects ({index, id, value, ts}).

examples.description: Read a string key and parse the JSON result.
examples.note: Prefer --json whenever the value will be parsed programmatically.

examples.description: Read a range of history entries by ID.
examples.note: Ranges and comma lists only apply to list and history keys; the result is an array of entries.

set.description: Set a value (string/counter), set field(s) on a state type, or batch set via --json.
set.agent_description: Write a string/counter value (one positional VALUE), set a state field (FIELD VALUE), or batch-set state fields with --json. Positionals also accept key=value pairs for batch state set.

args.name: args
args.description: Positional arguments: value (string/counter), field value (state), or key=value pairs (batch state set).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: JSON value: object for state batch, array for tensor positional set. Use "-" to read from stdin.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Link a memory entry (kn- ID) to this key, or "" to clear.

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Target a specific entry by ID (numeric or kv-HASH) for --memory.

examples.description: Set a string key to a single value.
examples.invocation: mx kv set 焦点 "ship the manifest CLI"
examples.note: String and counter keys take a single positional VALUE.

examples.description: Set one field of a state key (FIELD then VALUE).
examples.invocation: mx kv set deploy status green
examples.note: State keys take FIELD then VALUE as positionals; the key must already be of type state.

examples.description: Batch-set multiple fields of a state key via a JSON object.
examples.note: --json takes the value to write (use "-" to read it from stdin); it is an input, not an output toggle.

flags.name: --by
flags.description: Amount to increment by (default: 1).

examples.description: Increment a counter by 5.
examples.note: Omit --by to add 1. Only valid on counter keys.

flags.name: --by
flags.description: Amount to decrement by (default: 1).

push.description: Push a value onto a history or list.
push.agent_description: Append a value (optionally with structured --data) to a list/history; --create makes the key if missing.

flags.name: --data
flags.description: Attach structured JSON data to this entry.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Link a memory entry (kn- ID) to this entry.

flags.name: --create
flags.description: Auto-create key in schema if missing (type: history or list).

flags.name: --max-entries
flags.description: Maximum entries for the new key (only with --create).

examples.description: Append a finding to a history key, creating it if absent.
examples.invocation: mx kv push 知見 "clippy denies all warnings in CI" --create history
examples.note: Use --create the first time you write a key that is not yet in the schema.

examples.description: Push with attached structured data.
examples.invocation: mx kv push 倉庫 mx --data '{"host":"github","org":"yaoyorozu-industries"}'
examples.note: --where filters on search/last/count/random match these top-level data fields.

pop.description: Pop the last value from a list.

examples.description: Remove and return the last entry of a list key.
examples.note: Lists only — 'pop' on a history is a type_mismatch.

last.description: Get the last N entries from a history or list.

flags.name: --count
flags.description: Number of entries (default: 1).

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display the linked memory entry (kn- reference).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by structured data fields (key=value, top-level fields only, repeatable).

flags.name: --day
flags.description: Filter by specific day (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --month
flags.description: Filter by month (YYYY-MM, UTC).

flags.name: --week
flags.description: Filter by ISO week (YYYY-Www, e.g. 2026-W17).

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Start of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --to
flags.description: End of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Filter entries since a relative time (e.g. 30d, 1w, 2h) or ISO-8601 timestamp.

fields.name: ts
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

examples.description: Read the last 5 entries of a history key as JSON.
examples.note: Each entry carries index, id (hash), value, and ts.

since.description: Get history entries since a time reference (ISO-8601 or relative: 1h, 7d, 2w, 30m).

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display the linked memory entry (kn- reference).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

fields.name: ts
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

examples.description: Get history entries from the last 7 days as JSON.
examples.note: TIMEREF is a positional argument, not a flag.

search.description: Search entries in a list/history by substring and/or structured data filters.
search.agent_description: Find entries whose value contains a query string; combine with --where and date filters.

args.name: query
args.description: Search query (case-insensitive substring match on entry values).

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display the linked memory entry (kn- reference).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by structured data fields (key=value, top-level fields only, repeatable).

flags.name: --day
flags.description: Filter by specific day (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --month
flags.description: Filter by month (YYYY-MM, UTC).

flags.name: --week
flags.description: Filter by ISO week (YYYY-Www, e.g. 2026-W17).

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Start of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --to
flags.description: End of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Filter entries since a relative time (e.g. 30d, 1w, 2h) or ISO-8601 timestamp.

fields.name: ts
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

examples.description: Search a history key for entries mentioning a topic.
examples.note: Matching is a plain case-insensitive substring test, not a regex. QUERY is optional when filtering only by --where.

count.description: Count entries in a list/history, optionally filtered.

args.name: value
args.description: Count only entries matching this substring.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by structured data fields (key=value, top-level fields only, repeatable).

flags.name: --day
flags.description: Filter by specific day (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --month
flags.description: Filter by month (YYYY-MM, UTC).

flags.name: --week
flags.description: Filter by ISO week (YYYY-Www, e.g. 2026-W17).

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Start of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --to
flags.description: End of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Filter entries since a relative time (e.g. 30d, 1w, 2h) or ISO-8601 timestamp.

fields.name: latest_ts
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent matching entry.

examples.description: Count history entries as JSON.
examples.note: Pass a positional VALUE to count only entries containing that substring.

random.description: Get N random entries from a history or list.

flags.name: --count
flags.description: Number of random entries (default: 1).

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display the linked memory entry.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Output as JSON.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by structured data fields (key=value, top-level fields only, repeatable).

flags.name: --day
flags.description: Filter by specific day (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --month
flags.description: Filter by month (YYYY-MM, UTC).

flags.name: --week
flags.description: Filter by ISO week (YYYY-Www, e.g. 2026-W17).

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Start of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --to
flags.description: End of date range, inclusive (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Filter entries since a relative time (e.g. 30d, 1w, 2h) or ISO-8601 timestamp.

fields.name: ts
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

remove.description: Remove an entry by value or ID from a list/history.
remove.agent_description: Delete entries by --id (numeric index or kv- hash) or by substring VALUE; add --all to remove every match.

args.name: value
args.description: Value substring to match (omit if using --id).

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Remove by entry ID (numeric) or hash (kv-XXXX).

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Remove all matches (default: first match only).

examples.description: Remove a specific entry by ID.
examples.note: Use the positional VALUE form to match by substring instead; add --all to remove every match.

update.description: Update an existing entry's value and/or structured data in-place. Preserves the entry's ID, position, and timestamp.
update.agent_description: Edit an existing list/history entry in place by --id, changing its value text and/or merging structured --data. ID, position, and timestamp are preserved.

args.name: value
args.description: New value text (omit to update only --data).

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Target entry by ID (numeric index or kv-HASH prefix). Required.

flags.name: --data
flags.description: JSON object to merge into the entry's structured data. Null field values delete that field from the merged result.

examples.description: Rewrite an entry's value text in place.
examples.invocation: mx kv update 案件 --id 5 "new task description"
examples.note: --id is required. Omit the positional VALUE to update only --data.

examples.description: Merge structured data into an entry, deleting a field with null.
examples.invocation: mx kv update 倉庫 --id kv-2Bdsdz --data '{"status":"archived","stale":null}'
examples.note: Null field values delete that field from the merged data.

migrate.description: Migrate existing entries to match current schema data definitions.
migrate.agent_description: Reconcile a key's existing entries with the current schema's data definitions; --prune drops undeclared fields, --dry-run previews without writing.

flags.name: --prune
flags.description: Remove fields not declared in the current schema.

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would change without modifying data.

examples.description: Preview a schema migration without writing.
examples.note: Drop --dry-run to apply; add --prune to also remove fields no longer declared in the schema.

reset.description: Reset a key to its schema default.
reset.agent_description: Destructive: clears a key back to its schema default. Irreversible — there is no undo.

examples.description: Reset a list key back to its schema default (empty).
examples.note: Destructive and irreversible — confirm the key name before running.

dump.agent_description: Read every key and value at once; use --format compact for a one-line key=value layout.

flags.name: --format
flags.description: Output format: compact or json.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve and display linked memory entries (kn- references).

fields.name: _schema
fields.description: Name of the agent schema the state belongs to.

fields.name: _updated
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last state update.

examples.description: Dump all state as JSON (the default format).
examples.note: Use --format compact for a single line per key. Beyond _schema and _updated, every key appears as a top-level property.
